findings.issue: InvitationCompletePage.test.tsx 'shows error when invitation token is missing' test (line 103-116) renders at route /invitations/complete without :token param, which does not exist in the production AppRouter route config. In production, the pattern /invitations/:token/complete requires a token segment, so urlToken will never be undefined when InvitationCompletePage mounts. The test exercises unreachable defensive code (urlToken || retrieveInvitationToken() fallback) that cannot be triggered in production.
findings.remediation: Either remove this test (unreachable path) or document that it tests a defensive fallback for future route changes.

findings.issue: InvitationCompletePage.test.tsx 'shows error when stored verifier is missing' test (line 80-94) sets up redeemOAuth to resolve but the code path returns early at retrieveVerifier() check (line 43-46) before ever calling redeemOAuth. The mock setup is unused and misleading.
findings.remediation: Remove the redundant vi.mocked(redeemOAuth).mockResolvedValue(...) lines or replace with a comment noting that redeemOAuth is intentionally not called in this path.

findings.issue: makeRequest in utils.ts auto-attaches localStorage access_token as Bearer Authorization to ALL requests when no Authorization header is explicitly set. OAuth init and redeem calls (oauthApi.init, oauthApi.redeem) do not set an Authorization header, so a stale token from a previous session could be leaked to the OAuth endpoint. In practice this is harmless since the OAuth endpoints likely ignore unexpected auth headers, but the attachment is semantically incorrect for unauthenticated OAuth lifecycle calls.
findings.remediation: Consider adding an explicit skipAuth option to makeRequest for OAuth flows, or have oauthApi.init/redeem pass an empty Authorization header to suppress auto-attachment.
